purpose: Stabilize visual output across agents and human designers so that every new screen, mock, or component inherits the same atmosphere instead of reinventing one. This file is the structured entry; the canonical visual anchor is docs/design/visual-reference.html and the prose canon is docs/design/canon.md. All three move together.
authority_note: Nothing here can override spec/product-core.json, spec/interactions.json, spec/knowledge-map.json, spec/card-system.json, spec/action-surface.json, or spec/membership.json. When this file disagrees with an owner listed in spec/authority-map.json, the owner wins and this file is edited to match.

product_truth.note: Narrow by design. Only claims that, if violated, would corrupt product comprehension (library identity, self-assess semantics, Law of One) are product_truth. Everything visual that is merely a stylistic choice lives in implementation_hypothesis.

library_identity_is_visually_distinct.claim: Each CET library in spec/knowledge-map.json must be visually identifiable by a stable, learnable identity color so that the user always knows which library they are currently in.
library_identity_is_visually_distinct.mapping_stability: Once published to users, a library's assigned hue family must not be reassigned to another library in subsequent releases. The specific hex is implementation; the hue family (indigo / coral / mint / violet / cyan / rose / amber) becomes mnemonic and is therefore sticky.

law_of_one_current_library.claim: At most one library identity color may act as the dominant accent on a single screen. The current library drives CTA, picked, divider, highlight; the other libraries may only appear as small-scale chips or map tiles at ≤20% visual weight.

self_assess_feedback_scoped_to_flip.claim: The self-assess feedback color family is a 2-level light judgement (confident / review, rendered as 有把握 / 再回看) scoped to spec/interactions.json#flip.scoring_mode=light_self_assess. It must not leak into interactions whose scoring_mode is auto (multiple_choice, lock, elimination, swipe) — those render auto judgement instead. Two levels (not four) is the product_truth: spec/product-core.json#user_cost_minimization explicitly forbids '反复做高成本掌握度判断', and apps/mobile/src/learning/LearningSurface.tsx is the canonical implementation.

map_must_visualize_hierarchy.claim: The knowledge map surface must render the library → box → card hierarchy as visible spatial structure, not a flat list. This is the physical-space commitment from AP-02.

interaction_silhouettes_are_distinguisha.claim: The five core interactions (flip / multiple_choice / lock / elimination / swipe) must be distinguishable by screen silhouette when blurred — not by a shared card frame with swapped button icons.

implementation_hypothesis.note: All values below are stable defaults for agents and designers. They may be adjusted ±5% for individual components or micro-tuned by a design reviewer, but they should not be re-picked from scratch per screen. Concrete renderings live in docs/design/visual-reference.html.

self_assess_hex_defaults._note: Two levels, not four. 'review' is a warm amber (not red): it means '回来再看一次', not '错了/惩罚'.

palette.usage_constraint: Feedback hues must not be used as library identity. Library hues must not be used to encode correctness. Keep the two semantic families separate.

mono.usage: captions, specs, uppercase metadata, tabular numerals

typography.italic_accent_policy: Italic variant is used as editorial emphasis (lede-style), not as decoration. At most one italic run per hierarchy level.

glass_and_surfaces.edge_rim: 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.08) outer on night, 1px rgba(11,11,20,0.05) outer on day
glass_and_surfaces.shadow_policy: Day uses soft drop shadows for lift; night replaces shadows with rim-light dividers (top 1px highlight + bottom 1px lowlight).

motion.reduce_motion_fallback: disable aurora pulse, replace card flip with crossfade

device_frames.rationale: Matches iPhone 15/16 Pro. Using outdated ratios (390/844) makes mocks look like retro-fit stock templates.

component_vocabulary_rules: Use chips, not tag badges. Chips carry the library identity color in their left dot.
Tabbar is a floating VisionOS-style capsule; never a full-width bottom bar.
Progress is a single thin bar with tabular-numeric flanks; never circular dashboards on the main learning surface.
Dividers prefer spacing over lines; when a line is needed, it is 1px at ink_4 opacity 0.2.

taste_axes._note: Six pinned dimensions. These are not scores — they are directions. A design that drifts toward the anti_pole column is wrong by construction, regardless of how 'nice' it looks in isolation.

restraint_vs_expression.test: If you removed the decorative element, would the content still be understandable? If yes, remove it.

material_vs_flat.test: Glass surfaces must have measurable thickness: blur + 1px inner highlight + edge rim + 3-4% grain. Pure flat fills are forbidden on primary surfaces.

editorial_vs_marketing.test: No promotional language, no exclamation marks in chrome, no 'limited time' ribbons. Typography carries authority the way a book does, not the way a landing page does.

quiet_vs_loud.test: Max one strong accent per screen (Law of One). Max one motion loop visible at a time. No competing animations.

geometric_vs_organic.test: Display type is geometric sans (Outfit). No serifs, no handwritten scripts, no wavy dividers. Icons share a single stroke weight and join style.

sparse_vs_dense.test: If a screen needs a scroll just to finish the primary action, it is too dense. Whitespace is content.

interaction_silhouettes._note: When blurred, each of the five core interactions must be identifiable by its silhouette alone. These are the canonical shapes; bind to spec/interactions.json interaction ids.

flip.shape: single large centered card occupies ~60% vertical; below it a 2-slot pill row (confident / review); audio + hint live as inline chips on the card, not as separate surfaces.

multiple_choice.shape: prompt block top ~30%; 4 option tiles in a 2×2 grid below; the option grid is the silhouette — a single scrolling list of options is wrong.

lock.shape: vertical scrolling list of rows; each row has a leading lock icon; progress is implied by count of unlocked rows; no card frame around individual rows.

elimination.shape: grid or column of candidates (3–6); tap to strike through (strike-through is the key affordance); remaining candidates at end are the answer.

swipe.shape: deck of stacked cards with directional trail hints (left/right); exactly one card visible on top; horizontal motion is the primary affordance.

large_text_on_surface.standard: WCAG AA large (≥24px regular or ≥19px bold)

contrast_floor._note: Day and night both. When a chip/button uses library identity at low alpha, the text color inside must still clear the floor against the computed background, not against the base surface.

forbidden_design_patterns._note: Machine-checkable blocklist. Scope: any HTML/CSS fragment outside <!-- NEGATIVE --> ... <!-- /NEGATIVE --> blocks in docs/design/visual-reference.html. High-signal patterns only — broad words like 'streak' or raw '#000' are intentionally NOT listed because they have legitimate uses (daily-checkin counter, dynamic island, on-color text).

tokens.reason: gamification chrome forbidden by product-core; daily-checkin counters are fine, chrome is not
full-width bottom tabbar; use floating capsule
serifs forbidden in this product (geometric sans only)

design_review_checklist._note: Any agent producing visual output (mock, component, screen, reference HTML) must answer these before declaring work complete. 4 universal + 2 conditional. Answers go into the PR body or agent output; missing answers = incomplete work.

universal.question: Which library is the current one on this screen, and is the single strong accent bound to it? (Law of One)
What is the focal object, and is the first-read path (focal → secondary → chrome) clear when squinted?
Does the screen silhouette match the interaction's canonical silhouette in visual-language.json#interaction_silhouettes? If not, which interaction is it and why does the shape differ?
Any forbidden_design_patterns present? Gradient text, gamification chrome, full-width tabbar, pure #000/#fff, removed self-assess tokens, serifs?

conditional.condition: any rendered phone frame or constrained viewport
conditional.question: Layout containment: no horizontal overflow, safe-area respected, no clipped CTA or tabbar at target width?
Surface-specific: flip uses exactly 2 self-assess pills (有把握 / 再回看); stats uses tabular-nums; learning does not expose module-selection as primary path.
